user_query:
Please set up a lightweight, event-triggered funding alert workflow for our planning committee using the provided inputs under input/. When a new line with type "funding_check" is present in input/new_event.jsonl, the workflow should: (1) read input/projects.csv (primary_keywords and target_agencies are '|' separated), (2) perform live search engine queries to official sources (federal/state agencies such as USDOT, FTA, EPA, HUD, and state DOT or environmental departments) for current grant/NOFO/RFP opportunities that match each project's keywords and agencies, and (3) produce structured local outputs. Deliverables and constraints:

- Implement and run a simple command-line script saved as scripts/funding_watch.py that takes the event file and projects file and writes outputs to output/. Include the one-line run command you used at the top of output/status_update.md.
- Write an Internet search log to output/search_log.jsonl (JSON Lines). For each query run, capture: event_id, project_id, query, engine_name, timestamp_iso, and up to the top 3 results with {rank, title, domain}. Do not include raw URLs.
- Normalize and deduplicate findings into output/funding_candidates.json as an array of objects with fields: event_id, project_id, project_name, keyword, title, issuing_agency_guess, domain, gathered_at_iso. Limit to up to 5 candidate opportunities per project. issuing_agency_guess should be inferred from the result title or domain string where feasible.
- Compose a concise status summary in output/status_update.md that includes: (a) the exact one-line command to run the script in the future, (b) counts of candidates by project_id, and (c) a short narrative (3–5 sentences) highlighting notable opportunities.
- Edit input/memo_draft.md by inserting a new section titled "Funding Alerts" immediately after the "Upcoming Agenda Items" section. In that section, list each project and the titles and domains of its top 1–2 opportunities. Save the edited memo as output/meeting_memo_updated.md. Do not include URLs.
- Draft a notification email to planning@city.gov as output/email_draft.txt summarizing the top matches and request quick feedback. Use the event's deadline_hint_days from input/new_event.jsonl to compute a requested response-by date as today + deadline_hint_days (write the computed date in ISO format). Include a subject line and a brief body that references the event_id.
- Rewrite that email for tone and clarity as output/email_polished.txt: under 180 words, friendly-professional tone, with a crisp subject line and a short bullet list of the top 3 opportunities (title + domain only). No URLs.

Notes: input/new_event.jsonl already contains one new event line; use that as the trigger for this run. The Internet search step is required—use a search engine and official sources; do not rely on memory. Keep all outputs under output/, and do not include direct URLs anywhere (use titles and domains only). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
